title: Hammurabi's Code
description: The first time anyone wrote 'eye for an eye' on a giant rock and said this is the law now. Carved into stone so nobody could argue.

title: The Epic of Gilgamesh
description: The world's oldest story. A tyrant king and a wild man become best friends, kill a forest god, then chase immortality. It does not go well.

title: Sargon of Akkad
description: An abandoned baby floats down a river in a basket and grows up to build the world's first empire. Sound familiar?

title: Ashurbanipal's Library
description: A brutal Assyrian king collects every book in the known world. The first librarian was a war criminal.

title: The Hanging Gardens of Babylon
description: Nebuchadnezzar builds his homesick wife a man-made mountain of plants. We're still not 100% sure they actually existed.

title: Cyrus the Great
description: A Persian shepherd boy ends up freeing the Jews, conquering Babylon, and inventing human rights. Nobody saw it coming.

title: Darius Builds Persepolis
description: The Persian king builds a city so beautiful that Alexander burns it down 200 years later out of pure jealousy.

title: The Fall of Babylon
description: Cyrus diverts a river, walks his army into the city through the dry riverbed, and the party inside doesn't even notice until it's over.

title: Narmer Unites Egypt
description: One king, one crown, one giant slate showing him beating someone with a club. Welcome to Egypt.

title: Building the Pyramids
description: Tens of thousands of workers stack 2.3 million stone blocks for one guy's tomb. Still the most impressive flex in human history.

title: The Great Sphinx
description: A 240-foot lion with a Pharaoh's face stares into the desert for 4,500 years. Nobody is sure who built it or why.

title: Hatshepsut, the Woman Pharaoh
description: She crowns herself, wears a fake beard, rules for two decades, and builds half of Egypt. Then her stepson tries to erase her from history.

title: Akhenaten the Heretic
description: The Pharaoh who tried to delete every god except the sun. Egypt was so mad they tried to delete him right back.

title: Tutankhamun's Tomb
description: A teenage Pharaoh dies mysteriously, gets buried with a solid gold mask, and stays hidden for 3,300 years until a guy with a candle finds him.

title: Ramses the Great
description: Ruled for 66 years, fathered 100+ kids, fought the largest chariot battle in history, and put his face on every wall in Egypt.

title: The Battle of Kadesh
description: Egypt vs the Hittites, 5,000 chariots in the desert. Both sides went home and told everyone they won. The first peace treaty in history followed.

title: Cleopatra and Caesar
description: She has herself rolled up in a carpet and delivered to his bedroom. Within a year she's pregnant and Egypt has a new ally.

title: Cleopatra and Antony
description: Round two. She seduces Caesar's right-hand man, they have three kids and a fleet, and basically declare war on Rome together. Bold strategy.

title: The Battle of Actium
description: Cleopatra and Antony's combined fleet meets Octavian's at sea. Cleopatra panics, sails away, and Antony chases her. End of the Roman Republic.

title: The Death of Cleopatra
description: She locks herself in her tomb with a basket of figs and an extremely angry snake. The last Pharaoh of Egypt picks her own ending.

title: The Library of Alexandria Burns
description: The greatest collection of human knowledge ever assembled goes up in flames. We're still mad about it 2,000 years later.

title: The Trojan War
description: A prince steals another king's wife, so a thousand Greek ships sail to Troy and lay siege to its walls for ten blood-soaked years. Achilles rages, Hector dies, armies grind each other into the dust beneath the towers of Troy.

title: The Trojan Horse
description: Greeks pretend to give up, leave a giant wooden horse outside the gates, and the Trojans wheel it inside themselves. The original phishing attack.

title: Achilles and Hector
description: Greece's greatest warrior vs Troy's greatest prince. One has plot armor everywhere except his heel. You can guess how this ends.

title: Odysseus Goes Home
description: After ten years of war he tries to sail home. It takes another ten years, involves a Cyclops, a witch, and the dead. The original hero's journey.

title: Sparta Builds Its War Machine
description: A whole society where boys are taken from their mothers at 7 to be turned into killing machines. Spoiler: it works extremely well.

title: Draco Writes the Laws
description: An Athenian writes laws so harsh that almost every crime is punishable by death. We still call brutal rules 'draconian' because of him.

title: The Battle of Marathon
description: Outnumbered Athenians charge a much bigger Persian army and somehow win. A guy runs 26 miles to deliver the news, then drops dead.

title: The 300 at Thermopylae
description: King Leonidas and 300 Spartans hold a mountain pass against an entire Persian empire. They lose the battle but win the legend.

title: The Battle of Salamis
description: Greek triremes lure the Persian fleet into a narrow strait and obliterate them. Xerxes watches from a throne on a hill, horrified.

title: Pericles' Golden Age
description: Athens enters its glow-up era: democracy, the Parthenon, theatre, philosophy. Then a plague kills a third of the city, including Pericles.

title: The Peloponnesian War
description: 27 years of Athens vs Sparta. Plagues, betrayals, exile, and one of the most disastrous military expeditions in history. Athens loses everything.

title: Socrates Drinks the Hemlock
description: An old man asks too many questions in public. Athens votes to make him drink poison. He calmly does it while his students cry.

title: Plato Founds the Academy
description: The world's first university opens in an olive grove outside Athens. Lasts 900 years, invents most of Western philosophy.

title: Alexander the Great
description: By age 32 he's conquered most of the known world and reportedly cried because there was nothing left to conquer.

title: Alexander Cuts the Gordian Knot
description: Legend says whoever unties an impossible knot will rule Asia. Alexander pulls out his sword and cuts it in half. Cheating? Or genius?

title: The Death of Alexander
description: He drops dead at 32 in Babylon. His generals immediately rip the empire to shreds fighting over the corpse.

title: Archimedes Yells Eureka
description: A Greek genius figures out displacement in the bath, runs naked through the streets shouting about it. Later dies because he's too busy doing math to notice the Romans invading.

title: Romulus and Remus
description: Two abandoned brothers raised by a wolf decide to build a city. They argue about where. One murders the other. That city is Rome.

title: The Rape of Lucretia
description: A king's son assaults a noblewoman, she takes her own life, and Rome overthrows the monarchy forever. The Republic is born from one act of evil.

title: Cincinnatus Saves Rome
description: A retired farmer is begged to become dictator, defeats Rome's enemies in 16 days, then goes back to his plow. Power? No thanks.

title: The Gauls Sack Rome
description: A horde of Celts walks into Rome and burns it down. Geese famously save the last hill. Rome never forgets, and never forgives.

title: Hannibal Crosses the Alps
description: A Carthaginian general marches an army and 37 war elephants over snowy mountains to surprise-attack Rome from the north. Rome did not see it coming.

title: The Battle of Cannae
description: Hannibal surrounds and slaughters 60,000 Romans in a single afternoon. Still studied as the perfect battle 2,200 years later.

title: Carthage Must Be Destroyed
description: An old senator ends every speech with the same line until Rome finally levels Carthage and salts the earth. Petty? Yes. Effective? Also yes.

title: The Gracchi Brothers
description: Two brothers try to give land back to the poor. The Senate murders them both, one after the other. The Republic starts to crack.

title: Sulla Marches on Rome
description: A Roman general turns his army around and invades his own city. The first time it happens. Definitely not the last.

title: Spartacus Revolts
description: A Thracian gladiator and 70 friends escape a training school and end up leading 100,000 slaves in open war against Rome.

title: The First Triumvirate
description: Caesar, Pompey, and Crassus quietly carve up the Republic between themselves over wine. Three friends. Soon to be three enemies.

title: Caesar Conquers Gaul
description: Eight years, a million dead, another million enslaved. He writes his own bestselling book about it. The man knows branding.

title: Caesar Crosses the Rubicon
description: A general stands on the wrong side of a tiny river and the law says he's a traitor if he steps across it with his army. He does it anyway. 'Alea iacta est' — the die is cast. Civil war begins.

title: Caesar Crosses the Rubicon
description: Ordered to disband his army, Caesar instead marches it across a tiny river and starts a civil war. 'The die is cast.'

title: The Ides of March
description: Julius Caesar walks into the Roman Senate and never walks out. His closest friends turn on him in the most famous political betrayal in history. 'Et tu, Brute?'

title: Cicero's Death
description: Rome's greatest orator gets on the wrong side of Mark Antony. They cut off his head and hands and nail them to the Senate doors.

title: Augustus Becomes Emperor
description: Caesar's quiet 18-year-old nephew somehow outplays everyone, ends 100 years of civil war, and quietly turns Rome into an empire.

title: Varus Loses the Legions
description: Three Roman legions get ambushed in a German forest and wiped out. Augustus reportedly walks the palace banging his head, yelling 'Varus, give me back my legions!'

title: Tiberius on Capri
description: The emperor gets bored of Rome, moves to a private island, and lets his henchmen run the city. Things get extremely weird.

title: Caligula Goes Mad
description: He starts strong, gets a fever, and emerges convinced he's a god. Tries to make his horse a senator. Lasts three years before the guards stab him.

title: Claudius the Unlikely Emperor
description: Everyone thought the limping, stuttering uncle was harmless. Then the guards drag him from behind a curtain and crown him by accident. He's actually pretty good at it.

title: Boudicca's Revolt
description: A British queen leads 100,000 furious Celts and burns three Roman cities to the ground. Almost kicks Rome out of Britain entirely.

title: Nero Burns Rome
description: Half the city goes up in flames. Nero allegedly plays music while it burns, then blames Christians and starts the world's first crusade against them.

title: The Year of the Four Emperors
description: In 12 months, Rome cycles through four emperors. Three are murdered. The fourth, Vespasian, finally just shows up with an army and ends it.

title: The Destruction of Jerusalem
description: Vespasian's son Titus levels the Temple after a brutal siege. The Jewish diaspora begins. Rome builds an arch to brag about it.

title: Pompeii Destroyed
description: Mount Vesuvius erupts and buries an entire Roman city under 20 feet of ash in hours. We can still see the people frozen mid-step.

title: Trajan Builds the Empire
description: Rome hits its biggest size ever under a Spanish-born emperor who actually likes the people. Builds a column so tall you need binoculars to see the top.

title: Hadrian's Wall
description: Rome decides Britain north of a certain line is just not worth it and builds a 73-mile wall to keep the Picts out. Still standing.

title: Marcus Aurelius, Philosopher King
description: A Roman emperor writes a private journal about being a better person while fighting a 14-year war on the frontier. Still a bestseller.

title: Commodus the Gladiator Emperor
description: Marcus Aurelius's son thinks he's Hercules and fights in the Colosseum himself. His mistress and his trainer eventually strangle him in the bath.

title: The Crisis of the Third Century
description: 50 years, 26 emperors, plague, hyperinflation, barbarian invasions, and the empire splits into three. Rome should not have survived this.

title: Constantine Converts
description: Before a battle he sees a cross in the sky and a voice says 'in this sign, conquer.' He wins, and Christianity goes from persecuted cult to imperial religion.

title: The Council of Nicaea
description: Constantine locks 300 bishops in a room until they agree on what Christians actually believe. The result still gets recited in churches today.

title: Julian the Apostate
description: An emperor tries to undo Constantine's revolution and bring back the old gods. Lasts 20 months before dying in battle in Persia.

title: The Sack of Rome
description: The Visigoths walk into the Eternal City and loot it for three days. Rome hadn't been taken in 800 years. The world is in shock.

title: Attila the Hun
description: Sweeps out of the steppe and terrorizes both halves of the Roman empire. Dies on his wedding night from a nosebleed. Anticlimactic.

title: The Fall of Rome
description: A Germanic warlord politely deposes the last Roman emperor, a teenager named Romulus. 1,200 years end with a whimper.

title: Justinian and Theodora
description: An emperor marries a former actress, and together they save Constantinople from a riot, rebuild the legal code, and almost reconquer Rome.

title: The Nika Riots
description: Two rival sports fan groups team up and try to overthrow Justinian. Theodora tells him to stay and fight. 30,000 rioters die in a single day.

title: Hagia Sophia Rises
description: Justinian builds a dome so big and so daring that for 1,000 years nobody could match it. He looks up at it and says 'Solomon, I have outdone you.'

title: The Plague of Justinian
description: Bubonic plague hits Constantinople and kills 25-50 million people across the empire. The first pandemic on this scale in recorded history.

title: The Iconoclast Crisis
description: Two emperors decide religious art is a sin and order every icon in the empire smashed. The Church loses its mind. Lasts 100 years.

title: The Battle of Manzikert
description: Byzantine emperor walks into a Turkish ambush, loses everything, and the empire never recovers. The slow death of Byzantium begins.

title: The Fall of Constantinople
description: After 53 days of bombardment with the biggest cannons ever made, the 1,100-year-old city falls to the Ottoman Turks. The Middle Ages end here.

title: Muhammad's First Revelation
description: A 40-year-old merchant goes to a cave to think and an angel grabs him and tells him to recite. Islam begins.

title: The Hijra
description: Muhammad and his followers flee Mecca for Medina. The Islamic calendar starts from this exact moment.

title: The Battle of Badr
description: 313 outnumbered Muslims defeat 1,000 Meccans in the desert. Becomes the founding military legend of Islam.

title: The Battle of Karbala
description: The Prophet's grandson Hussein and 72 followers are killed in the desert by an Umayyad army. The wound that splits Islam into Sunni and Shia.

title: The Umayyads Build an Empire
description: Within 100 years of Muhammad's death, the Caliphate stretches from Spain to India. Largest empire the world had ever seen.

title: The House of Wisdom
description: Baghdad becomes the brain of the world: algebra, astronomy, medicine, every Greek text translated and improved. Then the Mongols torch it.

title: Saladin Retakes Jerusalem
description: After 88 years of Crusader rule, a Kurdish sultan walks back in. Famously refuses to massacre civilians, even though the Crusaders did exactly that in 1099.

title: The Mongols Sack Baghdad
description: The Tigris runs black with ink from books and red with blood. The Islamic Golden Age ends in a single week.

title: Charlemagne is Crowned
description: On Christmas Day, the Pope drops a crown on a Frankish king's head and declares the Roman Empire is back. Charlemagne pretends to be surprised.

title: The Battle of Tours
description: Charles Martel and the Franks stop a massive Umayyad invasion in central France. Basically decides whether Europe stays Christian.

title: The Vikings Raid Lindisfarne
description: Long ships appear out of the fog, monks get slaughtered in their own monastery, and Europe learns a new word: Viking.

title: Alfred the Great
description: An English king on the run from the Vikings hides in a swamp, regroups, and ends up the only English ruler called 'the Great.'

title: The Battle of Hastings
description: William of Normandy invades England, fakes a retreat, and an arrow goes through King Harold's eye. England is never the same.

title: The First Crusade
description: The Pope calls for holy war. 60,000 Europeans march 2,000 miles, take Jerusalem, and massacre everyone inside. The blood reportedly came up to the horses' knees.

title: Thomas Becket Murdered
description: King Henry II yells 'will no one rid me of this turbulent priest?' Four knights take him literally and murder the archbishop in his own cathedral.

title: Richard the Lionheart
description: An English king who barely speaks English spends his entire reign on Crusade or in prison. His brother John runs the country into the ground at home.

title: The Magna Carta
description: Furious barons corner King John in a meadow and force him to sign away his absolute power. The first paper limit on a king. Ever.

title: The Fourth Crusade Sacks Constantinople
description: Crusaders heading for Jerusalem run out of money, get hired by Venice, and sack the biggest Christian city in the world instead.

title: The Templars Destroyed
description: The richest knights in Europe get arrested in one coordinated raid on Friday the 13th. Their leader is burned alive. The French king takes their gold.

title: The Black Death
description: A plague riding on rats and fleas wipes out a third of Europe in four years. People paint dancing skeletons everywhere.

title: The Hundred Years' War
description: England and France fight over the French throne for 116 years. Longbows, knights, plague, and Joan of Arc. Nobody really wins.

title: Joan of Arc
description: An illiterate teenage farmgirl says God told her to lead the French army. She does. The English burn her alive at 19. Centuries later they make her a saint.

title: The Wars of the Roses
description: Two branches of the same English royal family kill each other for 32 years over which color rose is better. Game of Thrones is basically this.

title: Genghis Khan Rises
description: An abandoned boy on the Mongolian steppe unites every warring tribe and ends up conquering more land than any human in history.

title: Kublai Khan Founds the Yuan
description: Genghis's grandson finishes the job in China and rules from a city Marco Polo calls the most magnificent on earth.

title: Marco Polo in China
description: A Venetian teenager travels the Silk Road, lives in Kublai Khan's court for 17 years, and comes home to a Europe that thinks he's making it all up.

title: The Kamikaze Saves Japan
description: The Mongols try to invade Japan twice. Both times a typhoon shows up and shreds the fleet. The Japanese name the wind 'divine.'

title: The Ming Dynasty Rises
description: A Chinese peasant overthrows the Mongols and founds a dynasty that will rebuild the Great Wall and run the world's biggest fleet.

title: Zheng He's Treasure Fleet
description: A Chinese eunuch admiral leads 300 ships and 28,000 men across the Indian Ocean. Then China decides exploring isn't worth it and burns the fleet.

title: The Tale of Genji
description: A noblewoman in Kyoto writes the world's first psychological novel about a beautiful prince and his messy love life. 1,000 years before the form catches on.

title: The Samurai Code
description: Japan's warrior class decides loyalty, honor, and a clean sword cut are more important than life itself. Then they enforce it for 700 years.

title: Angkor Wat Built
description: A Khmer king builds the largest religious monument on earth, then loses it to the jungle for 400 years.

title: The Delhi Sultanate
description: A former slave founds a Muslim dynasty in India that lasts 320 years and includes a sultan who reportedly tried to move the capital to a different city. Twice.

title: The Medici Rise
description: A family of bankers takes over Florence, funds the Renaissance, and produces three popes and two queens of France. All from interest.

title: Greek Scholars Flee West
description: After Constantinople falls, refugee Greek scholars pour into Italy carrying ancient manuscripts. The Renaissance suddenly has rocket fuel.

title: Gutenberg Prints the Bible
description: A German tinkerer builds the printing press, mass-produces the Bible, and accidentally invents mass communication, science, and revolution.

title: Savonarola's Bonfire of the Vanities
description: A Dominican monk takes over Florence and burns books, art, and makeup in a giant pile. Then Florence burns him.

title: The Borgia Popes
description: A Spanish family buys the papacy and uses it to commit poisoning, incest rumors, and political assassinations. Cesare Borgia inspires Machiavelli.

title: Leonardo da Vinci
description: Paints the Mona Lisa, designs flying machines, dissects corpses, writes backwards. One man casually invents the future in his spare time.

title: Michelangelo and the Sistine Chapel
description: He hates painting, hates the Pope, and lies on his back for four years anyway. Result: the most famous ceiling on earth.

title: Machiavelli Writes The Prince
description: A fired Florentine bureaucrat writes a how-to manual for ruthless rulers, hoping to get his job back. Five centuries later it's still required reading.

title: Luther's 95 Theses
description: A German monk nails a list of complaints to a church door. Within a few years half of Europe has broken with Rome.

title: Henry VIII and His Six Wives
description: Wants a son so badly he breaks with the Pope, beheads two wives, divorces two more, and ruins his own legacy. Get him out of his marriage.

title: Anne Boleyn Beheaded
description: She made a king break with Rome to marry her. Three years later he had her head cut off with a French sword for a fake adultery charge.

title: Bloody Mary
description: Henry VIII's Catholic daughter takes the throne and burns 280 Protestants alive trying to drag England back to Rome. Dies childless five years later.

title: Elizabeth I
description: The 'Virgin Queen' rules England for 45 years, never marries, defeats the Spanish Armada, and turns a backwater into a superpower.

title: Mary Queen of Scots
description: Three husbands, multiple plots, 19 years in English prison, and finally beheaded by her own cousin Elizabeth. The most cinematic monarch ever.

title: The Spanish Armada
description: Spain sends 130 ships to invade England. Storms, fireships, and English cannons send most of them to the bottom of the sea.

title: The Gunpowder Plot
description: Guy Fawkes and friends try to blow up Parliament with the king inside. They get caught the night before. The UK still burns Fawkes in effigy every year.

title: Columbus Sails the Ocean Blue
description: An Italian sailor convinces Spain to fund a shortcut to India. He misses by an entire continent, calls the locals 'Indians,' and changes the world.

title: Vasco da Gama Reaches India
description: A Portuguese captain finally finds the actual sea route to India. Returns home with cargo worth 60 times the cost of the voyage.

title: Magellan Circumnavigates the Globe
description: He sets out with 5 ships and 270 men. Three years later, 1 ship and 18 starving survivors limp back. Magellan is not one of them.

title: Cortés Conquers the Aztecs
description: 600 Spaniards walk into the Aztec capital, take the emperor hostage, and burn their own ships so nobody can retreat. Two years later, the empire is gone.

title: The Fall of Tenochtitlan
description: After a brutal 75-day siege, the largest city in the Americas falls. Mexico City rises on its bones.

title: Pizarro and the Inca
description: 168 Spaniards walk up the Andes, kidnap the Inca emperor in front of his army, and take a room full of gold as ransom. Then kill him anyway.

title: Atahualpa's Ransom
description: The Inca emperor fills a giant room twice with gold and silver to buy his freedom. The Spanish take the gold and execute him anyway.

title: Drake Circumnavigates
description: An English pirate-captain robs Spanish ships all the way around the world and comes home loaded with treasure. Elizabeth I knights him on his own deck.

title: Jamestown Founded
description: 104 Englishmen show up in Virginia thinking they'll find gold. Most starve to death. The few who survive accidentally start America.

title: The Mayflower Lands
description: Religious separatists aiming for Virginia get blown off course and land in Massachusetts. Half of them die that first winter.

title: The Thirty Years' War
description: What starts as Catholics vs Protestants in Bohemia ends with most of Central Europe in ruins and 8 million dead. Religion was just the excuse.

title: Galileo's Trial
description: He says the Earth orbits the Sun. The Church says recant or die. He recants, mutters 'and yet it moves' on the way out, and gets house arrest forever.

title: The English Civil War
description: King Charles I tries to rule without Parliament. Parliament raises an army. Nine years later the king's head rolls off a block in front of his own palace.

title: The Execution of Charles I
description: First time in history a European king is publicly tried, convicted, and executed by his own people. Even his enemies are shocked.

title: Cromwell Rules
description: An English Puritan general bans Christmas, theatre, and basically having fun. After he dies, the country digs him up just to behead his corpse.

title: Louis XIV Builds Versailles
description: The Sun King turns a hunting lodge into a 2,000-room palace and forces every powerful French noble to live there so he can keep an eye on them.

title: The Great Fire of London
description: A bakery fire spreads for four days and burns down 13,000 houses and 87 churches. Silver lining: kills off the plague rats too.

title: Newton's Apple
description: A young Cambridge student goes home during plague lockdown and basically invents calculus, optics, and gravity in 18 months.

title: The Salem Witch Trials
description: Two girls in colonial Massachusetts start having fits and accusing their neighbors. 19 people get hanged before the adults come to their senses.

title: Peter the Great
description: A 6'8 Russian tsar travels Europe undercover, learns shipbuilding, comes home, shaves all the boyars' beards by force, and builds St. Petersburg in a swamp.

title: Catherine the Great
description: A German princess marries the wrong tsar, has him deposed (and possibly killed), then rules Russia for 34 years and almost doubles its size.

title: Voltaire and the Philosophes
description: A bunch of French intellectuals get exiled, locked up, and bestselling for arguing that maybe kings and priests aren't always right.

title: The Seven Years' War
description: The first true world war. Britain vs France in Europe, North America, India, and the Caribbean. Britain wins, gets Canada, and accidentally bankrupts itself.

title: The Boston Tea Party
description: Colonists dressed as Mohawks dump 342 chests of British tea into Boston Harbor. The most expensive tantrum in history.

title: The Declaration of Independence
description: Thirteen tiny colonies write a breakup letter to the world's biggest empire. 'All men are created equal' becomes a problem they don't fully solve.

title: Washington Crosses the Delaware
description: On Christmas night, in a snowstorm, Washington rows across an icy river to ambush drunk Hessian troops. Saves the entire Revolution.

title: The Surrender at Yorktown
description: Trapped between Washington's army and the French navy, the British surrender. The band reportedly plays 'The World Turned Upside Down.'

title: Storming the Bastille
description: A Paris mob breaks into a royal prison, finds only seven prisoners (none of them political), and kicks off the French Revolution anyway.

title: Marie Antoinette's Execution
description: The queen who probably never said 'let them eat cake' is paraded through Paris in a cart and beheaded. She apologizes to the executioner for stepping on his foot.

title: The Reign of Terror
description: The French Revolution starts eating its own. Robespierre sends thousands to the guillotine, then it's his turn. The blade is impartial.

title: The Haitian Revolution
description: Enslaved people in France's richest colony rise up, defeat Napoleon's army, and found the first free Black republic. Then France charges them for it.

title: Napoleon's Rise
description: A short Corsican gunner artillery officer climbs from nothing to Emperor of France in ten years. Then conquers most of Europe to celebrate.

title: Napoleon Crowns Himself
description: At his own coronation, Napoleon takes the crown out of the Pope's hands and puts it on his own head. The Pope is just there for decoration.

title: The Battle of Trafalgar
description: Admiral Nelson destroys the combined French and Spanish fleet, saving Britain from invasion. He gets shot dead in the moment of victory.

title: The Battle of Austerlitz
description: Three emperors, one battlefield. Napoleon lures Russia and Austria onto frozen lakes, blows up the ice with cannons, and wins his masterpiece.

title: Napoleon Invades Russia
description: He marches in with 600,000 men. The Russians burn everything as they retreat. Six months later 100,000 frozen survivors stagger out.

title: The Battle of Waterloo
description: Back from exile, Napoleon rolls the dice one last time. Wellington and the Prussians end his career on a muddy Belgian field in a single afternoon.

title: Napoleon at St. Helena
description: Britain dumps Napoleon on a tiny rock in the South Atlantic. He spends six years complaining about the weather and dies of stomach cancer (or arsenic).

title: The War of 1812
description: The British invade Washington and burn down the White House. Andrew Jackson wins New Orleans two weeks after the war is technically over.

title: Simón Bolívar Liberates Latin America
description: One man crosses the Andes, leads armies for 16 years, and frees six countries from Spanish rule. Then dies disillusioned and broke.

title: The Monroe Doctrine
description: The young United States tells Europe to stay out of the entire Western Hemisphere. Bold for a country that can barely defend its own coast.

title: The Trail of Tears
description: The U.S. forces 60,000 Native Americans off their ancestral land and marches them west. Thousands die on the way. A national wound that never heals.

title: Remember the Alamo
description: 200 Texans hold a Spanish mission against thousands of Mexican troops for 13 days. Everyone inside dies. Texas wins the war anyway.

title: The Opium Wars
description: China tries to stop British merchants from flooding the country with opium. Britain sends gunboats. China is forced to keep buying drugs at gunpoint.

title: The Irish Potato Famine
description: A potato disease wipes out the food of an entire country. A million Irish die, a million flee. Britain ships food out the whole time.

title: The 1848 Revolutions
description: Almost every European capital catches fire at once. Kings flee, constitutions are signed, and a year later most of it is rolled back.

title: The California Gold Rush
description: A guy finds gold at Sutter's Mill. Within two years, 300,000 people are scrambling across the continent for it. San Francisco goes from village to city.

title: The Taiping Rebellion
description: A failed civil servant decides he's Jesus's brother and raises a 30 million-strong rebellion. Maybe the deadliest civil war ever fought.

title: The Crimean War
description: Britain, France, and the Ottomans fight Russia over a holy site. Florence Nightingale invents modern nursing in the chaos.

title: Perry Opens Japan
description: An American admiral sails black warships into Tokyo Bay and politely asks Japan to start trading. After 250 years of isolation, Japan says yes at gunpoint.

title: The Sepoy Mutiny
description: Indian soldiers in the British East India Company revolt over rifle cartridges greased with cow and pig fat. The British Crown takes over India directly.

title: Darwin Publishes the Origin
description: A quiet English naturalist publishes a book about finches and accidentally rewrites how humans understand themselves.

title: Italian Unification
description: Garibaldi and his red-shirted thousand sail south, conquer Sicily and Naples, and hand it all to a king. Italy becomes a country for the first time.

title: German Unification
description: Bismarck stitches together 39 squabbling German states by starting and winning three wars in a row. Crowns the King of Prussia in Versailles for spite.

title: Lincoln is Elected
description: An anti-slavery lawyer from Illinois wins the presidency and seven Southern states immediately walk out. The Union starts to break.

title: Fort Sumter Falls
description: Confederate cannons open fire on a federal fort in Charleston Harbor. 34 hours later, the fort surrenders. The Civil War has begun.

title: The Emancipation Proclamation
description: Lincoln declares enslaved people in rebel states to be free. The war is no longer about preserving the Union. It's about ending slavery.

title: The Battle of Gettysburg
description: Three days, 50,000 casualties, and Pickett's Charge into a meat grinder. The South never recovers. Lincoln gives a 272-word speech about it.

title: Sherman's March to the Sea
description: A Union general burns a 60-mile-wide path of destruction from Atlanta to Savannah to break the South's will to fight. It works.

title: Lee Surrenders at Appomattox
description: Two generals meet in a farmhouse parlor. Grant lets Lee's men keep their horses and go home. The war is finally over.

title: Lincoln is Assassinated
description: Five days after Appomattox, an actor sneaks into the President's box at Ford's Theatre and shoots him in the head. The country never recovers.

title: The Scramble for Africa
description: European powers gather around a map in Berlin and carve up an entire continent over brandy. They draw lines that still cause wars today.

title: The Battle of Isandlwana
description: Zulu warriors with spears destroy a fully equipped British army. Britain is so humiliated it spends the rest of the year hunting down a king.

title: Rorke's Drift
description: 150 British soldiers hold a tiny mission station against 4,000 Zulus through the night. Eleven Victoria Crosses awarded. Britain calls it victory.

title: The Meiji Restoration
description: Japan ends 700 years of samurai rule, restores the Emperor, and modernizes faster than any country ever has. In 30 years it goes from feudal to industrial.

title: The Battle of Little Bighorn
description: Custer charges a vastly larger Lakota and Cheyenne force and gets every single one of his men killed. Sitting Bull's last great victory.

title: Wounded Knee
description: U.S. cavalry massacres about 250 Lakota men, women, and children in a South Dakota creek bed. The end of the Indian Wars.

title: The Spanish-American War
description: The USS Maine explodes in Havana harbor. Newspapers blame Spain. Four months later America has Cuba, Puerto Rico, the Philippines, and Guam.

title: The Boxer Rebellion
description: A Chinese martial arts cult tries to expel all foreigners. Eight foreign armies march into Beijing together to crush it. China is humiliated again.

title: The Russo-Japanese War
description: Tiny modernized Japan annihilates a Russian fleet at Tsushima. First time in centuries an Asian power beats a European one. Russia is so humiliated it has a revolution.

title: The Titanic Sinks
description: The 'unsinkable' ship hits an iceberg on its maiden voyage. Not enough lifeboats. 1,500 people die in the freezing North Atlantic.

title: Archduke Franz Ferdinand is Shot
description: A teenager in Sarajevo shoots an Austrian heir on the world's worst-routed motorcade. Six weeks later, the entire planet is at war.

title: The Christmas Truce
description: On Christmas Eve in the trenches, German soldiers start singing carols. British soldiers join in. They climb out and play soccer. The next day they're shooting again.

title: Gallipoli
description: An Allied plan to knock the Ottomans out of the war turns into eight months of carnage on a Turkish beach. Australia and New Zealand are still grieving.

title: The Lusitania Sinks
description: A German U-boat torpedoes a passenger liner off Ireland. 1,200 dead, including 128 Americans. America starts thinking about joining the war.

title: The Battle of Verdun
description: Ten months of meat-grinder warfare for almost no ground. 700,000 casualties. The bones are still being collected.

title: The Battle of the Somme
description: On day one, 19,000 British soldiers die. Most of them in the first hour. The battle drags on for four more months.

title: The Russian Revolution
description: A bread riot in Petrograd brings down the tsar in a week. Lenin returns from exile in a sealed train. Russia is suddenly Soviet.

title: The Tsar and His Family Are Shot
description: Nicholas II, his wife, four daughters, and son are taken to a basement in Yekaterinburg and executed. The Romanov dynasty ends after 304 years.

title: The Treaty of Versailles
description: The Allies blame Germany for everything and stick it with crippling reparations. One French marshal says: 'This is not peace. It is an armistice for 20 years.'

title: The Spanish Flu
description: A virus rides the troopships home and kills 50 million people in two years. More than the war that just ended.

title: The Roaring Twenties
description: Jazz, flappers, bootleg gin, and a stock market that only goes up. Everyone's having fun until they're not.

title: The Stock Market Crashes
description: On Black Tuesday, the market loses billions in a single day. Bankers jump out of windows. The world plunges into the Great Depression.

title: Hitler Rises to Power
description: A failed Austrian painter and former corporal becomes Chancellor of Germany through the ballot box. Within months he has absolute power.

title: Stalin's Purges
description: Stalin executes or exiles his own officers, party members, scientists, even photographers who took the wrong photo. Millions disappear.

title: Mao's Long March
description: Chinese Communists retreat 6,000 miles through mountains and deserts to escape destruction. Only 1 in 10 survive. Mao emerges as undisputed leader.

title: The Spanish Civil War
description: Fascists, Republicans, anarchists, and communists tear Spain apart for three years. Hitler and Mussolini test their bombers on civilians.

title: The Munich Agreement
description: Britain and France hand Hitler a chunk of Czechoslovakia in exchange for a promise. Chamberlain comes home waving a paper: 'Peace for our time.' It is not.

title: The Invasion of Poland
description: On September 1, German tanks pour across the border. Two days later, Britain and France declare war. WWII has begun.

title: The Fall of France
description: German blitzkrieg goes around the Maginot Line. France falls in six weeks. Hitler dances a little jig in front of the Eiffel Tower.

title: The Dunkirk Evacuation
description: Trapped against the sea, 338,000 British and French soldiers are rescued by a flotilla of Navy ships and civilian fishing boats. A defeat that feels like a miracle.

title: The Battle of Britain
description: RAF pilots in Spitfires hold off the German air force in the skies over England. Churchill: 'Never was so much owed by so many to so few.'

title: Operation Barbarossa
description: Hitler invades the Soviet Union with 3 million men. The largest invasion in history. He thinks it'll take three months. It takes everything he has.

title: Pearl Harbor
description: On a quiet Sunday morning, Japanese carriers launch a surprise attack on the U.S. Pacific Fleet. America wakes up at war.

title: The Holocaust
description: Nazi Germany industrially murders six million Jews and millions of others. The darkest organized crime in human history.

title: The Battle of Midway
description: American codebreakers know exactly where Japan is going. U.S. dive bombers sink four Japanese carriers in five minutes. The Pacific war turns.

title: The Battle of Stalingrad
description: Five months of street-to-street fighting in a frozen ruined city. Two million casualties. Germany's army gets surrounded and surrenders.

title: D-Day
description: On June 6, 156,000 Allied troops storm five beaches in Normandy. The largest amphibious invasion ever. The beginning of the end for Hitler.

title: The Warsaw Uprising
description: The Polish Home Army rises up to free their own capital. They hold out for 63 days. The Red Army across the river watches and does nothing.

title: The Battle of the Bulge
description: Hitler's last big gamble. He hits the Allies in the freezing Ardennes during Christmas. Surrounded in Bastogne, the Americans answer surrender demands with one word: 'Nuts.'

title: The Fall of Berlin
description: The Red Army storms the Reichstag. Hitler shoots himself in a bunker beneath the city. The Soviet flag goes up over Berlin.

title: Hiroshima and Nagasaki
description: Two atomic bombs vaporize two Japanese cities in three days. Over 200,000 people die. Japan surrenders. The nuclear age begins.

title: The Nuremberg Trials
description: Top Nazi leaders are put on trial in their own former rally city. 'I was just following orders' is rejected as a defense. Modern war crimes law is born.

title: The Iron Curtain Falls
description: Churchill warns the world that Stalin has dropped a curtain across Europe. The Cold War is officially named.

title: The Partition of India
description: Britain leaves India in a hurry. A line is drawn on a map by a man who's never been there. 15 million people flee in both directions and a million die.

title: The Founding of Israel
description: Three years after the Holocaust, the State of Israel is declared. Five Arab armies invade the next day. The conflict has not ended since.

title: The Berlin Airlift
description: Stalin blockades West Berlin. The U.S. and Britain fly in 2.3 million tons of supplies, sometimes one plane every 30 seconds, for almost a year. Stalin gives up.

title: Mao Founds the People's Republic
description: After 22 years of civil war, Mao stands on a balcony in Beijing and declares China is now Communist. The losing side flees to Taiwan.

title: The Korean War
description: The North invades the South. The U.S. counterattacks. China sends a million 'volunteers.' Three years later, everyone stops on roughly the same line they started on.

title: The Hungarian Revolution
description: Hungarians topple the Soviet-backed government in 13 days. Then 200,000 Soviet troops and 2,500 tanks roll in. The West does nothing.

title: Sputnik Launches
description: A beach ball-sized Soviet satellite goes 'beep' from orbit. America panics, dumps money into NASA, and the Space Race is on.

title: The Cuban Missile Crisis
description: Soviet nukes appear 90 miles off the Florida coast. For 13 days, JFK and Khrushchev play chicken with the end of the world. Khrushchev blinks first.

title: JFK is Assassinated
description: In Dallas, Texas, the President is shot from a book depository window. The accused shooter is killed two days later on live TV. The conspiracy theories never stop.

title: Martin Luther King's 'I Have a Dream'
description: On the steps of the Lincoln Memorial, MLK delivers the most famous speech in American history. 250,000 people listen in silence.

title: The Vietnam War
description: The U.S. spends 20 years, 58,000 lives, and immense moral capital trying to stop South Vietnam from going Communist. It goes Communist anyway.

title: The Moon Landing
description: On July 20, Neil Armstrong steps off a ladder onto another world. 600 million people on Earth hold their breath. 'One small step…'

title: Watergate
description: A bungled break-in at a Washington hotel ends with a U.S. President resigning before he can be impeached. Two journalists become legends.

title: The Fall of Saigon
description: American helicopters lift the last people off the embassy roof while North Vietnamese tanks crash through the gates. The Vietnam War ends.

title: The Iran Hostage Crisis
description: Iranian students storm the U.S. embassy and hold 52 Americans for 444 days. A failed rescue mission ends in flames in the desert.

title: The Fall of the Berlin Wall
description: An East German official misreads a memo on live TV. Within hours, thousands of Berliners are tearing the wall down with hammers. The Cold War is essentially over.

title: Tiananmen Square
description: Chinese troops clear a protest in the heart of Beijing with tanks and live rounds. One man stands alone in front of a column of tanks. Nobody knows his name.

title: The Soviet Union Collapses
description: On Christmas Day, the hammer and sickle is lowered over the Kremlin for the last time. 15 new countries are born overnight.

title: Mandela Walks Free
description: After 27 years in prison, Nelson Mandela walks out of Victor Verster prison and into history. Four years later he's President of South Africa.

title: The Rwandan Genocide
description: In 100 days, neighbors murder neighbors with machetes. 800,000 dead. The world watches and does almost nothing.

title: The Yugoslav Wars
description: A multi-ethnic country tears itself apart along religious and ethnic lines. The word 'genocide' returns to Europe for the first time since WWII.

title: September 11
description: Hijacked planes hit the Twin Towers and the Pentagon. A fourth crashes in a Pennsylvania field. The world after this morning is a different one.

title: The Iraq War
description: The U.S. invades Iraq looking for weapons that aren't there. Topples Saddam in three weeks. Spends the next decade trying to leave.

title: The 2008 Financial Crisis
description: Wall Street's bet on bad mortgages blows up. Lehman Brothers vanishes overnight. The whole global economy nearly goes with it.

title: The Arab Spring
description: A Tunisian street vendor sets himself on fire. Within months, dictators across the Arab world are falling. Then it gets complicated.

title: The COVID-19 Pandemic
description: A virus from a market in Wuhan locks down the entire planet. Millions die. The world learns the word 'unprecedented' the hard way.
